pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states L1 should have 'clear visibility (10nm)'. Verified against task.json description field, line 21: 'Daytime (10:00), clear visibility (10nm), calm weather (0), 2 traffic vessels.'
Task description explicitly states L1 should have 'calm weather (0)'. Verified in task.json description.
Task description explicitly states L1 should have '2 traffic vessels'. Verified in task.json description.
Task description explicitly states L2 should have 'reduced visibility (8nm)'. Verified in task.json description.
Task description explicitly states L2 should have 'moderate weather (3)'. Verified in task.json description.
Task description explicitly states L2 should have '4 traffic vessels'. Verified in task.json description.
Task description explicitly states L3 should have 'poor visibility (1.5nm)'. Verified in task.json description.
Task description explicitly states L3 should have 'rough weather (5)'. Verified in task.json description.
Task description explicitly states L3 should have '6 traffic vessels'. Verified in task.json description.

Official Bridge Command documentation (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/config.php) confirms full_radar is a valid configuration parameter: 'If set to 1, Bridge Command will start with the radar maximised.'

Official Bridge Command documentation (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/config.php) confirms arpa_on is a valid configuration parameter: 'If set to 1, Bridge Command will start the radar with ARPA active.'

Parameter not found in official Bridge Command documentation (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/config.php), bc5.ini INI file format specification (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/4.1/IniFileFormat.php), or any official Bridge Command source references. Web searches return only general radar theory, not Bridge Command-specific documentation.

Parameter not found in official Bridge Command documentation. Official docs reference RadarRange(1), RadarRange(2), etc. indexed parameters with values in nautical miles (e.g., RadarRange(1)=12), not a single max_radar_range parameter. This parameter name does not appear in any official documentation.

Task description explicitly states 'Lat 50.7-50.8' for valid own ship coordinates. Metadata claims 50.65-50.90 which is WIDER. Web search (https://latitude.to/articles-by-country/gb/united-kingdom/6146/solent) confirms Solent center: 50.7853. Description bounds (50.7-50.8) are narrower and more accurate for Solent proper than metadata (50.65-50.90).
Task description explicitly states 'Lat 50.7-50.8'. Metadata claims 50.90 which exceeds the task requirement. Description bounds are the canonical constraint in the task specification.

Task description explicitly states 'Long -1.3 to -1.0' for valid own ship coordinates. Metadata claims -1.50 to -0.95 which is WIDER. Web search confirms Solent center: -1.2903. Description bounds (-1.3 to -1.0) are narrower and more accurate than metadata.
Task description explicitly states 'Long -1.3 to -1.0'. Metadata claims -0.95 which extends beyond the task requirement.

pi_items.verified_value: Rule 5 - Look-out (requires proper look-out by sight and hearing)
pi_items.source: Official IMO (International Maritime Organization) COLREGS documentation (https://www.imo.org/en/About/Conventions/Pages/COLREG.php). Rule 5 is part of Convention on the International Regulations for Preventing Collisions at Sea, 1972.

pi_items.verified_value: Rule 6 - Safe Speed (requires vessels to proceed at safe speed)
pi_items.source: Official IMO COLREGS documentation. Rule 6 is a fundamental collision prevention regulation.

pi_items.verified_value: Rule 7 - Risk of Collision (requires use of available means to determine risk)
pi_items.source: Official IMO COLREGS documentation. Rule 7 mandates careful assessment of collision risk.

pi_items.verified_value: Rule 19 - Conduct of Vessels in Restricted Visibility
pi_items.source: Official IMO COLREGS documentation. Rule 19 specifies conduct for vessels in fog, mist, or other restricted visibility conditions.

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic task using Bridge Command (open-source ship bridge simulator)
pi_items.source: Task uses Bridge Command simulator as specified in env.json (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk). The three scenarios are synthetic training scenarios created for this task, not real data.

privileged_info_summary: The progressive_training_suite task requires creating three progressive training scenarios in Bridge Command's Solent environment. L1 should have 10nm visibility, calm weather (0), and 2 traffic vessels. L2 should have 8nm visibility, moderate weather (3), and 4 traffic vessels. L3 should have 1.5nm visibility, rough weather (5), and 6 traffic vessels. Own ship must be placed at coordinates within Lat 50.7-50.8, Long -1.3 to -1.0 (NOT the wider bounds in metadata: 50.65-50.90 and -1.50 to -0.95). Radar must be configured with full_radar=1 and arpa_on=1. A 500+ word syllabus must reference COLREGS Rules 5, 6, 7, and 19, which are real maritime collision prevention regulations from IMO. NOTE: The metadata contains discrepancies - it specifies wider Solent coordinate bounds (50.65-50.90, -1.50 to -0.95) than the task description requires (50.7-50.8, -1.3 to -1.0), and includes two undocumented radar parameters (radar_range_resolution=256, max_radar_range=72) that do not appear in official Bridge Command documentation.
